title: What is Java? ☕
description: Discover the language powering billions of devices.

slides.title: Meet Java! 👋
slides.content:
# Welcome to Java!

Java is one of the most popular programming languages in the world. It was created in 1995 and is known for its **"Write Once, Run Anywhere"** philosophy.

From Android apps to large enterprise servers, and even the Mars Rover—Java is everywhere!

slides.title: Why Learn Java? 🌟
slides.content:
## The Power of Java

1. **High Demand**: Huge job market for Java developers.
2. **Platform Independent**: Write it on a Mac, run it on Windows, Linux, or a phone.
3. **Object-Oriented**: Helps you organize code like real-world objects.
4. **Massive Community**: Millions of developers to help you if you get stuck.

quizData.question: What is Java's famous philosophy?
quizData.explanation: Java programs are compiled into bytecode, which can run on any device equipped with a Java Virtual Machine (JVM). This makes it platform-independent!

slides.title: How Java Works ⚙️
slides.content:
## Under the Hood

When you write Java code, it goes through a two-step process:

1. **Compilation**: Your readable code is translated into **Bytecode** by the Java Compiler.
2. **Execution**: The **Java Virtual Machine (JVM)** interprets this Bytecode and runs it on your specific device.

slides.title: The JVM is the Secret Sauce 🤫
slides.content: The JVM is what makes "Run Anywhere" possible. As long as a device has a JVM installed, it can understand and run Java Bytecode!

quizData.question: What translates your written Java code into Bytecode?
quizData.explanation: The Java Compiler is responsible for translating your human-readable Java code into Bytecode. The JVM then runs that Bytecode.

slides.title: Ready to code? 🛠️
slides.content:
## You're all set!

You now know what Java is and how it runs. In the next lesson, we will get our hands dirty and write some actual Java code! Get ready to say hello to the world.
